headline: Model Avalanche: 12+ AI Releases in One Week, Anthropic Hits Capacity Limits, and Claude Goes Rogue

stories.title: OpenAI Kills Sora, Launches GPT-5.4 with Wild 1M Token Context
stories.summary: OpenAI's shutting down Sora mid-April citing unsustainable compute costs, but fr the real move is GPT-5.4—1.05M token context, 33% fewer hallucinations vs 5.2, new Tool Search feature that's actually clever for agents.
stories.takeaway: The era of image generation as a product is over; reasoning + agents are where the money is. Tool Search cuts costs on complex workflows by dynamically fetching tool defs instead of dumping them all in the prompt.

stories.title: Anthropic's Claude Hits Capacity Wall, Leaks New Model "Mythos" Above Opus
stories.summary: Claude paid subs just hit record numbers but Anthropic's infrastructure is crying uncle—peak hour limits tightened Mar 26 during 8am-2pm ET. Plus draft blog posts leaked showing a new tier called Mythos/Capybara with "dramatically higher" benchmark scores.
stories.takeaway: Anthropic's success is becoming its own bottleneck. New model teased will likely be ultra-expensive given the naming pattern. Meanwhile Claude Code is lowkey eating everyone's lunch—300% usage growth since Claude 4 launch.

stories.title: MCP Hits 97M Installs as Every AI Lab Standardizes on Agent Protocol
stories.summary: Model Context Protocol crossed 97M installs in March, went from niche Anthropic thing to de facto standard in like 16 months. OpenAI, Google, xAI, Mistral all shipping MCP tooling. Over 4K published servers now covering every SaaS + enterprise system.
stories.takeaway: Agent infrastructure is solidifying. Anyone building agents needs to understand MCP or you're gonna be irrelevant fast. This isn't hype—it's boring infrastructure that actually works.

stories.title: 12 Models Dropped in One Week—GPT-5.4, Gemini 3.1 Ultra, Qwen 3.5 Small, All Fighting for Mindshare
stories.summary: March 10-16 was absolutely unhinged—12 meaningfully different models across text, code, image, audio from OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, xAI, Mistral. Qwen 3.5 9B hitting 81.7% on GPQA Diamond (vs GPT-OSS 120B's 71.5%). Literally the gap between proprietary and open is months now, not years.
stories.takeaway: Incremental updates are done. It's all about specialized models now—one for reasoning, one for efficiency, one for coding. Selection paralysis is real; devs freezing upgrades for 2 weeks to wait for benchmarks.

watchNext.title: Xiaomi's Stealth MiMo-V2-Pro Model Gets Exposed After Tricking Community with 'Hunter Alpha' Beta
watchNext.why: Lowkey the wildest story—Xiaomi ran a secret trillion-token model through OpenRouter for weeks pretending it was DeepSeek. Community thought they had V4 early. Wild flex on how fragmented the market is.

watchNext.title: Google's Gemini API Agent Skill Jumps Success Rate from 28% to 96.6% on Coding Tasks
watchNext.why: Super tactical—Google built a skill that feeds agents up-to-date SDK info so they don't hallucinate outdated APIs. Simple idea, massive impact. This is how you actually make agents work.
